news.text: JPMorgan's Dimon warns Iran war may drive inflation and interest rates higher - Reuters
North Korea distancing itself from Iran to leave door open for US talks, Seoul says - Reuters
Iran has formulated its response to ceasefire proposals, foreign ministry spokesperson says - Reuters
Iran accuses UN nuclear watchdog of inaction, warns of risk from attacks - Reuters
Intelligence head of Iran's Revolutionary Guards killed in strike, media say - Reuters
How is South Asia tackling the Iran war-driven energy crisis? - Reuters

brief:
◆ Market Overview

Markets opening cautiously mixed as Iran tensions dominate headlines. SPY flat at $655.83 (+0.09%) while VIX remains elevated at 34.7, signaling continued uncertainty. Small caps showing relative strength with IWM up 0.69% as traders position defensively.

◆ What to Watch

  • Iran Headlines: Every news wire matters today - ceasefire talks vs. escalation rhetoric driving intraday moves
  • Energy Sector: XLE up 0.47% pre-market, watch crude oil futures for continuation
  • Treasury Yields: Holding steady but Dimon's inflation warning could spark bond selling
  • Earnings Light: Only small names reporting - FAT and KALA after hours, both expected deep red
⚠ Bear Case
JPMorgan's Dimon warning on war-driven inflation could trigger rate fears and tech selling. Consumer Discretionary already down 1.50% pre-market signals spending concerns.

◆ Sector Rotation

Classic risk-off rotation playing out with Real Estate (+1.61%) and Utilities (+0.50%) leading as bond proxies. Tech hanging tough at +0.80% despite rate concerns, while Consumer Discretionary getting hammered at -1.50%. Energy benefiting from war premium but gains remain modest.
⚡ Key Driver
Iran ceasefire response expected today. Any hint of de-escalation crushes energy/defense plays while boosting growth stocks. Escalation does the opposite.
✦ Bull Case
Small cap strength (IWM +0.69%) suggests domestic focus over international risks. VIX decline from higher levels indicates some fear premium coming out.

◆ Levels That Matter

  • SPY 650: Critical support - break below triggers broader selling into 640s
  • QQQ 590: Resistance level - clear break above signals tech leadership return
  • VIX 30: Below this level and fear trade unwinds quickly
  • XLE 95: Energy breakout level if Iran tensions persist

Not a prediction. A preparation. Make your plan before the bell.
